# emem — Earth memory protocol

> Working memory of Earth, for AI agents. Every patch of ground is an address
> (cell64, ~9.55 m), every measurement there is a signed fact, every read is a
> content-addressed receipt your code can verify offline. The address space is
> the planet; the state is persistent; the bytes are reproducible across any
> replica that mirrors it.
>
> Where most agent runtimes ship per-session associative memory or per-tenant
> scratchpads, emem is the layer underneath: shared, cross-session,
> cross-tenant, persistent working memory of the world itself. Calls are
> idempotent; the CID is the citation two agents paste at each other to stop
> arguing about a number.
>
> Bootstrap: GET /v1/discover  ·  Schema: /openapi.json  ·  MCP: /mcp

## Algebra

    Fact     :: Cell × Band × Tslot → {value, unit, provenance, fact_cid}
    Receipt  :: Request → {fact_cids, responder_pubkey, sig}
    cell64   = b1024(lat[21] · lng[22])    pitch ≈ 9.55 m at equator
    CID      = base32-nopad-lowercase(blake3(canonical_cbor(fact)), 32B)
    sig      = ed25519(blake3(request_id ‖ served_at ‖ primitive ‖ cells ‖ fact_cids))

## Install (one line each)

    Claude/Cursor/Cline/VSCode MCP : { "mcpServers": { "emem": { "url": "https://emem.dev/mcp" } } }
    Anthropic API                  : { "mcp_servers": [{ "type": "url", "url": "https://emem.dev/mcp", "name": "emem" }] }
    Gemini CLI                     : gemini extensions install https://emem.dev/gemini-extension.json
    OpenAI Custom GPT Action       : import https://emem.dev/openapi.action.json   (auth: none)

## Primitives (typed)

    ask              :: Question × (Place|Cell) → Fact[] × Algorithm[] × Receipt
    locate           :: (Place|LatLng)          → Cell × Bbox
    recall           :: Cell × Band[]           → Fact[] × Receipt
    recall_polygon   :: (Place|Bbox) × Band[]   → Fact[][] × Aggregate × Receipt
    compare          :: Cell × Cell × Family    → Score × Receipt
    find_similar     :: Cell × Embedding × k    → (Cell × cos)[k] × Receipt
    diff             :: Cell × Tslot × Tslot × Band[] → Δ × Receipt
    trajectory       :: Cell × Band × [t0,t1]   → Fact[] × Receipt
    backfill         :: Cell × Band × [t0,t1]   → Fact[] × Receipt
    verify_receipt   :: Receipt                 → {valid: bool, signer}

Full descriptors with input schemas: GET /v1/agent_card  ·  POST /mcp tools/list

## Executable examples

    # ask — free-text → topic-route → recall → algorithms (one call)
    curl -sX POST https://emem.dev/v1/ask \
      -H 'content-type: application/json' \
      -d '{"q":"air quality in new delhi today","place":"New Delhi"}'

    # recall — cell × bands → signed facts
    curl -sX POST https://emem.dev/v1/recall \
      -H 'content-type: application/json' \
      -d '{"cell":"defi.zb592.nemu.zEvE","bands":["copdem30m.elevation_mean"]}'

    # recall_polygon — region question (locate → polygon → fan-out)
    curl -sX POST https://emem.dev/v1/recall_polygon \
      -H 'content-type: application/json' \
      -d '{"place":"Lagos","bands":["cams.pm25"]}'

    # find_similar — k-NN over a foundation embedding
    curl -sX POST https://emem.dev/v1/find_similar \
      -H 'content-type: application/json' \
      -d '{"key":"defi.zb592.nemu.zEvE","band":"geotessera","k":5}'

    # MCP tools/list
    curl -sX POST https://emem.dev/mcp \
      -H 'content-type: application/json' \
      -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"tools/list"}'

## Hunter mode

    hunt :: Event × (Region|PolygonBbox) → Hotspot[8] × Receipt[]

Twelve event keywords trigger an event-discovery sweep over a named
region: algal_bloom, deforestation, flood_extent, wildfire,
urban_heat_island, methane_plume, landslide, drought, soil_salinity,
crop_stress, water_turbidity, oil_slick. The responder samples up to
32 cells from the region polygon (8 for slow primary bands such as
MODIS LST), recalls the algorithm's primary scalar plus any
configured gate band (NDWI > 0 for over-water phenomena), and ranks
the cells. Tessera embedding rerank fires when ≥3 cells have
geotessera vectors. oil_slick has no algorithm registered yet — the
responder honestly returns status: not_yet_implemented with pointers
at flood_extent_sar_threshold@1 and water_turbidity_red_band@1.

    curl -sX POST https://emem.dev/v1/hunt \
      -H 'content-type: application/json' \
      -d '{"event":"algal_bloom","region":"Lake Erie"}'

Free-text questions of the form `find <event> in <region>` route
through /v1/ask's classifier and land on the same path.

## EUDR Due Diligence Statements

    eudr_dds :: Plot[] × CutOffDate → Annex II envelope × Receipt[]

Regulation (EU) 2023/1115 plot-level compliance under the regulation
as written. Forest definition follows Article 2(4): >0.5 ha, >5 m
tree height, >10 % canopy cover, excluding land predominantly under
agricultural or urban use. Cut-off date is 2020-12-31 per Article 1(2).

The endpoint applies eudr_compliance@1 per cell across the plot
polygon, then aggregates with a 0.1 % de-minimis threshold. Article
2(28) dispatches POINT (≤4 ha non-cattle) vs POLYGON (>4 ha or any
cattle plot). Forest baseline is JRC GFC2020 V3 (the EU Commission's
expected non-binding baseline) confirmed against Hansen GFC v1.12
treecover2000 >= 10 %. JRC Tropical Moist Forest annual change,
deforestation year, degradation year, and transition subtype layer
in via a pull-and-cache connector (JEODPP serves 84 MB tiles without
HTTP Range, so the responder caches once into $EMEM_DATA/jrc_tmf_cache/
with atomic rename). Sims et al. 2025 driver attribution and RADD SAR
alerts surface as refinement when wired; today both materialize signed
Absence with a structured NotImplemented reason rather than fabricate
a value the responder cannot read.

    curl -sX POST https://emem.dev/v1/eudr_dds \
      -H 'content-type: application/json' \
      -d '{"plots":[{"plot_id":"farm-001","geometry_geojson":{"type":"Polygon","coordinates":[[[-60.5,-3.5],[-60.4,-3.5],[-60.4,-3.4],[-60.5,-3.4],[-60.5,-3.5]]]},"country_of_production":"BRA","commodity_hs":"0901","commodity_name":"coffee","quantity_kg":12000}],"operator":{"name":"Acme Coffee BV","eori":"NL123456789"}}'

The structured Annex II JSON schema lives at /v1/schemas/eudr_dds.json
with $comment fields citing the exact EUR-Lex paragraph each field
maps to. EUDR Article 9(1)(b) legality verification (land tenure, FPIC,
country-of-origin laws) is structurally out of Earth-observation scope;
every response carries an explicit `legality_disclaimer` for that reason.
Pair with a partner legality module before submitting to TRACES NT.

## Instructions

Behavioural rules an agent should follow when using emem (Stripe convention,
present-tense, imperative, ≤8 lines):

    1. Resolve a place name with /v1/locate before /v1/recall. Don't fabricate cell64s.
    2. Cite receipt.fact_cids[0] in the first 13 chars (a CID prefix). Never invent a CID.
    3. Surface Fact::Absence as "no answer here", not as a missing field. Don't retry.
    4. Treat 404 cid_not_found as "call /v1/recall first to materialise"; the responder's hint says so.
    5. Verify receipts offline before quoting them — see /skills.md § verify-receipt-offline.
    6. Mention responder_pubkey_b32 once per session so the user knows who signed.
    7. Compose primitives (locate → recall, recall → verify, recall_polygon → solve) — don't reinvent.
    8. When in doubt, GET /v1/agent_card or /v1/discover; the responder is self-describing.

## When NOT to use emem

Pattern-match these question shapes and skip the call. Each is paired
with the better-fit tool so an agent that filters here keeps the
downstream answer grounded rather than punting.

    "is it raining right now in <place>"        → met.no, NOAA, Open-Meteo (real-time / sub-daily)
    "forecast 6 months out for <place>"          → out of horizon (JEPA v2 short-circuits past ~2 weeks)
    "sub-metre building footprint at <place>"    → Planet Pelican / Maxar (10 m is emem's native pitch)
    "land price / carbon credit price at <place>"→ market data services (emem signs Earth observation, not economics)
    "live air quality reading at <place>"        → PurpleAir / OpenAQ (cams.* is global model output, not sensor)
    "stream the latest tile of <band>"           → upstream STAC API (emem is snapshot-at-tslot, not streaming)
    "what's inside this building"                → indoor mapping is out of scope
    "verify EUDR Article 9(1)(b) legality"       → partner legality module (land tenure / FPIC / country-of-origin is not EO)
    "submit my DDS to TRACES NT"                 → operator's own EU Login + EORI flow (emem produces the payload, not the submission)

## Citation contract

Every numeric response carries: `value`, `unit`, `band_metadata.{interpretation,
pitfalls, dimension_description}`, `value_decoded` (categorical), `signer_pubkey_b32`,
`fact_cid`, `receipt.responder_pubkey_b32`. Cite `receipt.fact_cids[0]` (13-char
prefix). Mention `responder_pubkey_b32` once per session. Never invent a CID.

## Skills

Composed recipes (locate+recall, find_similar+verify, recall_polygon+solve, ...)
are documented as a flat cookbook at /skills.md and shipped as installable
[Anthropic Skills](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills) at
github.com/Vortx-AI/emem/tree/main/claude-skills:

    emem-locate-and-recall   place name → cell64 → signed facts
    emem-verify-receipt      BLAKE3 + Ed25519 in-process, no callback
    emem-find-similar        top-K cosine over the 128-D Tessera embedding
    emem-recall-polygon      every cell inside a polygon, per-cell receipts

Install in Claude Code by copying the bundle into your project's .claude/skills/.

## Guarantees

    hash_alg        : blake3
    sig_alg         : ed25519
    encoding        : cbor (canonical, deterministic)
    auth            : none for L0/L1 read paths; attester key for L2 writes
    absence         : signed; do not retry — surface as honest gap
    idempotent      : recall, compare, find_similar, diff, verify_receipt
    deterministic   : identical canonical facts → identical CID across responders
    auto-materialize: recall miss on a wired band fetches from open-data upstream + signs

## Catalogs (machine-readable)

    /v1/bands              — band ontology, dims, tempo, materializer status
    /v1/algorithms         — composition recipes (flood_risk, walkability, …)
    /v1/materializers      — wire-stable list of auto-materializing bands
    /v1/data_availability  — per-band kind/tempo/history-window
    /v1/coverage_matrix    — has_materializer, facts_count, last_attested
    /v1/fleet              — sensor lineage (S1/S2/MODIS/Cop-DEM/…)
    /v1/grid_info          — cell64 pitch + DGGS interop (h3, s2)

## Discovery

    /v1/discover                    — typed bootstrap (manifests, primitives, ontology)
    /openapi.json                   — full OpenAPI 3.1 spec
    /.well-known/agent-card.json    — A2A v0.2 agent card
    /.well-known/mcp.json           — MCP server descriptor
    /.well-known/emem.json          — manifest CIDs + responder pubkey
    /agents.md                      — full integration guide (consumer agents)
    /skills.md                      — composed recipes (cookbook view)
    /whitepaper.md                  — architecture + math
    /humans                         — interactive console; every /v1/* call prints in a live log pane
    /docs/gallery                   — live coverage map + per-place scenes + 32 protocol & industry diagrams
    /docs/diagrams/                 — the 32 SVG diagrams, byte-stable, embed-anywhere
    /v1/coverage_map.svg            — global coverage as a 1440×720 plate-carrée SVG
    /v1/places/scene_overlay.svg    — per-place value-painted polygon overlay (any scalar band)
